verdict_reasoning: FN1 (fibronectin-1) is a large, secreted extracellular matrix (ECM) glycoprotein that is stably deposited on the external surface of cells and in the surrounding tissue matrix. While synthesized intracellularly and secreted via the classical ER-Golgi pathway, FN1 is not a soluble secreted protein that freely diffuses; it is actively assembled into insoluble ECM fibrils via integrin-mediated cell-surface interaction and covalent cross-linking. The protein body reaches and persists on the cell surface and in extracellular matrix via stable, wash-resistant attachment to cell-surface integrins (α5β1, αvβ3, and others) and to other ECM components. This mechanism permits direct access by extracellular binders (antibodies, small-molecule integrin modulators, peptide mimetics, and biologics targeting fibronectin-integrin interaction). FN1 is a validated therapeutic target (e.g., anti-FN1 antibodies, integrin-targeting agents). The stable ECM residence and integrin-tethered PM pool make FN1 accessible from the extracellular space.
